useCase.name: One-stop digital public services
useCase.problem: People are forced to move between disconnected portals, offices, and payment channels to complete a single public service.
useCase.outcome: A person can start one service journey, verify identity once, submit only required data, pay or receive funds, and track status.

summary: Ethiopia: A person can start one service journey, verify identity once, submit only required data, pay or receive funds, and track status.

recommendedDpgs.name: Apache APISIX
recommendedDpgs.reason: Apache APISIX maps to data exchange needs for one-stop digital public services.

recommendedDpgs.name: MOSIP
recommendedDpgs.reason: MOSIP maps to identity needs for one-stop digital public services.

countrySystems.name: MESOB one-stop service platform
countrySystems.note: Citizen-facing service gateway and service orchestration layer.

countrySystems.name: MESOB Pay / wallet roadmap
countrySystems.note: Future sovereign payment gateway or wallet for public service flows.

countrySystems.name: APISIX integration gateway
countrySystems.note: API routing, authorization, audit logging, and ministry data access.

countrySystems.name: Community Service Centers
countrySystems.note: Assisted access points for people who cannot use digital channels alone.

countrySystems.name: Fayda Digital ID
countrySystems.note: Authentication and remote identity verification for service users.

countrySystems.name: OpenAgriNet
countrySystems.note: Agriculture records and service integration opportunity.
